steps.title: Identify the punctuation pattern
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'stratigraphy—the': Opens an em dash pair. The closing dash already appears after "(strata)", so this correctly sets off the supplementary phrase "the analysis of Earth's sediment layers (strata)" between two dashes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'stratigraphy (the': Opens a parenthesis, but the closing punctuation is a dash — you cannot pair an open parenthesis with a dash. ✗
  • Choice C — 'stratigraphy: the': A colon cannot be paired with a closing dash to set off a supplementary element. ✗
  • Choice D — 'stratigraphy, the': A comma cannot be paired with a closing dash in this way. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only a matching em dash pair can frame the supplementary definition "the analysis of Earth's sediment layers (strata)" correctly within the sentence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
